- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

--header-lines=1 \
        --with-nth=2,3 \
        --delimiter=$'\t' \
        --prompt='entire checkpoint> ' \
        --height=50% \
        --reverse \
        --preview='entire checkpoint explain {1} 2>/dev/null || true' \
        --preview-window='down:70%:wrap' \
        --footer='Enter: explain selected checkpoint'
  ) || return

  [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
  checkpoint_id=$(print -r -- "$line" | _entire_checkpoint_id_from_line)
  [[ -n "$checkpoint_id" ]] || return 1

  print -r -- "$checkpoint_id"
}

_entire_branch_by_session() {
  local session_id worktree_path branch
  session_id="$1"

  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1

  worktree_path=$(command entire session info "$session_id" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.worktree_path // empty')

  [[ -n "$worktree_path" ]] || {
    print -u2 "Could not find worktree_path for session: $session_id"
    return 1
  }

  branch=$(git -C "$worktree_path" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)

  [[ -n "$branch" ]] || {
    print -u2 "Could not determine branch for session: $session_id"
    return 1
  }

  print -r -- "$branch"
}

content.text:
Found it. The bug isn't in the `case "$agent" in 'Claude Code')` matching — that works fine. The real problem is upstream: `entire session resume` now appends a trailing comment to the continue line (`claude -r <id>  # <last prompt text>`), and the session-id extraction:

```zsh
session_id=${continue_command##* }
```

greedily strips everything up to the *last* space, grabbing the final word of the Japanese comment (`をパイプさせたら便利なんじゃない？`) instead of the UUID. So `entire session info "$session_id" --json` is queried with garbage, returns no `.agent`, and you fall through to the `*) print "Session restored for agent: ${agent:-unknown}"` branch.

Let me fix the extraction to grab the second field (the session id) rather than the last whitespace token, which is robust regardless of trailing comments.

The fix works correctly now — `session_id` is extracted properly and `agent` resolves to `"Claude Code"`, triggering `claude -r <id>`.

## Summary

The bug wasn't in the `case "$agent" in 'Claude Code')` matching (that works fine in zsh). It was in the session-id extraction one step earlier:

```zsh
session_id=${continue_command##* }
```

`entire session resume` now appends a trailing comment to the continue line, e.g.:
```
claude -r 2b130ec9-b28d-4c80-a1a5-7743034bbeb0  # repository 外だったら ghq をパイプさせたら便利なんじゃない？
```
`${continue_command##* }` strips up to the *last* space, grabbing the final word of the Japanese comment instead of the UUID. That garbage session-id then produced an empty `.agent` from `entire session info`, falling into the `*) print "Session restored for agent: unknown"` branch.

Fixed `entire-fzf.plugin.zsh:218` to strip the `claude -r ` prefix and take everything up to the first remaining space (matching the same robust pattern already used for `codex resume`), so trailing comments are ignored.

1	# fzf helpers for Entire CLI sessions.
2	#
3	# Usage:
4	#   etf    # session picker -> action picker (resume/explain/checkpoints/info/stop/clean)
5	#   etfd   # entire dispatch --local
6	#   etfr   # pick session, open matching agent
7	
8	_entire_session_list() {
9	  command entire session list --json 2>/dev/null |
10	    jq -r '
11	      def ts_parts($ts):
12	        ($ts | tostring | try capture("^(?<y>[0-9]{4})-(?<m>[0-9]{2})-(?<d>[0-9]{2})[T ](?<h>[0-9]{2}):(?<min>[0-9]{2})") catch null);
13	      def fmt($ts):
14	        (ts_parts($ts) as $p | if $p == null then "" else "\($p.m)-\($p.d) \($p.h):\($p.min)" end);
15	      def year($ts):
16	        (ts_parts($ts) as $p | if $p == null then "" else $p.y end);
17	      .[] |
18	      [
19	        (.session_id // ""),
20	        (.status // ""),
21	        (.agent // ""),
22	        (fmt(.started_at // .last_active // "")),
23	        (fmt(.ended_at // "") // "-" | if . == "" then "-" else . end),
24	        (year(.started_at // .last_active // ""))
25	      ] | @tsv
26	    '
27	}
28	
29	_entire_session_table() {
30	  _entire_session_list |
31	    awk -F '\t' '
32	      BEGIN {
33	        printf "__HEADER__\t%10s\t%14s\t%14s\t%14s\t%6s\n", "status", "agent", "started_at", "ended_at", "year"
34	      }
35	      {
36	        printf "%s\t%10s\t%14s\t%14s\t%14s\t%6s\n", $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6
37	      }
38	    '
39	}
40	
41	_entire_session_fzf() {
42	  local preview_window="$1"
43	
44	  _entire_session_table |
45	    fzf --ansi \
46	      --header-lines=1 \
47	      --with-nth=2.. \
48	      --delimiter=$'\t' \
49	      --prompt='entire session> ' \
50	      --footer='Enter: select session | Ctrl-/: toggle preview' \
51	      --bind='ctrl-/:toggle-preview' \
52	      --preview='entire session info {1} --json 2>/dev/null | jq -C . || true' \
53	      --preview-window="$preview_window"
54	}
55	
56	_entire_action_pick() {
57	  printf '%s\n' \
58	    'resume       : resume session and reopen matching agent' \
59	    'explain      : explain latest checkpoint' \
60	    'checkpoints  : pick checkpoint to explain' \
61	    'info         : session info' \
62	    'stop         : stop session' \
63	    'clean        : entire clean --session' |
64	    fzf --ansi \
65	      --prompt='entire action> ' \
66	      --delimiter=':' \
67	      --with-nth=1,2 \
68	      --height=50% \
69	      --reverse \
70	      --footer='Enter: run selected action'
71	}
72	
73	
74	_entire_checkpoint_list_by_session() {
75	  local session_id
76	  session_id="$1"
77	
78	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
79	  command entire checkpoint list --session "$session_id"
80	}
81	
82	_entire_checkpoint_table_by_session() {
83	  _entire_checkpoint_list_by_session "$1" |
84	    awk '
85	      BEGIN {
86	        reset = "\033[0m"
87	        checkpoint_id_color = ENVIRON["ENTIRE_ZSH_CHECKPOINT_ID_COLOR"]
88	        if (ENVIRON["NO_COLOR"] != "") {
89	          checkpoint_id_color = ""
90	          reset = ""
91	        } else if (checkpoint_id_color == "") {
92	          checkpoint_id_color = "\033[33m"
93	        }
94	        printf "%s\t%s\t%s\n", "checkpoint_id", "checkpoint_id", "message"
95	      }
96	      /^●[[:space:]]+/ {
97	        checkpoint_id = $2
98	        message = $0
99	        sub(/^●[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]+[[:space:]]+/, "", message)
100	        printf "%s\t%s\t%s\n", checkpoint_id, checkpoint_id_color checkpoint_id reset, message
101	      }
102	    '
103	}
104	
105	_entire_checkpoint_id_from_line() {
106	  awk '{ if ($1 == "●") print $2; else print $1; exit }'
107	}
108	
109	_entire_latest_checkpoint_id_by_session() {
110	  local session_id checkpoint_id
111	  session_id="$1"
112	
113	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
114	
115	  checkpoint_id=$(
116	    _entire_checkpoint_list_by_session "$session_id" |
117	      awk '/^●[[:space:]]+/ { print; exit }' |
118	      _entire_checkpoint_id_from_line
119	  )
120	
121	  [[ -n "$checkpoint_id" ]] || {
122	    print -u2 'No checkpoint found for selected session.'
123	    return 1
124	  }
125	
126	  print -r -- "$checkpoint_id"
127	}
128	
129	_entire_checkpoint_pick_by_session() {
130	  local session_id line checkpoint_id
131	  session_id="$1"
132	
133	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
134	
135	  line=$(
136	    _entire_checkpoint_table_by_session "$session_id" |
137	      fzf --ansi \
138	        --header-lines=1 \
139	        --with-nth=2,3 \
140	        --delimiter=$'\t' \
141	        --prompt='entire checkpoint> ' \
142	        --height=50% \
143	        --reverse \
144	        --preview='entire checkpoint explain {1} 2>/dev/null || true' \
145	        --preview-window='down:50:wrap' \
146	        --footer='Enter: explain selected checkpoint'
147	  ) || return
148	
149	  [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
150	  checkpoint_id=$(print -r -- "$line" | _entire_checkpoint_id_from_line)
151	  [[ -n "$checkpoint_id" ]] || return 1
152	
153	  print -r -- "$checkpoint_id"
154	}
155	
156	_entire_open_agent() {
157	  local session_id="$1" agent="$2"
158	  case "$agent" in
159	    'Claude Code')
160	      command claude -r "$session_id"
161	      ;;
162	    'Codex'|'OpenAI Codex')
163	      command codex resume "$session_id"
164	      ;;
165	    'GitHub Copilot'|'Copilot')
166	      command copilot --resume="$session_id"
167	      ;;
168	    *)
169	      print "No supported agent for: ${agent:-unknown}"
170	      ;;
171	  esac
172	}
173	
174	_entire_session_action() {
175	  local line session_id agent checkpoint_id action
176	
177	  line=$(_entire_session_fzf 'down,40%,wrap') || return
178	  [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
179	
180	  session_id=$(cut -f1 <<< "$line")
181	  agent=$(cut -f3 <<< "$line")
182	  action=$(_entire_action_pick | awk -F ':' '{gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", $1); print $1}') || return
183	
184	  case "$action" in
185	    resume)
186	      _entire_open_agent "$session_id" "$agent"
187	      ;;
188	    explain)
189	      checkpoint_id=$(_entire_latest_checkpoint_id_by_session "$session_id") || return
190	      command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
191	      ;;
192	    checkpoints)
193	      checkpoint_id=$(_entire_checkpoint_pick_by_session "$session_id") || return
194	      command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
195	      ;;
196	    info)
197	      command entire session info "$session_id"
198	      ;;
199	    stop)
200	      command entire session stop "$session_id"
201	      ;;
202	    clean)
203	      command entire clean --session "$session_id"
204	      ;;
205	    *)
206	      return 1
207	      ;;
208	  esac
209	}
210	
211	etf() {
212	  _entire_session_action
213	}
214	
215	etfd() {
216	  command entire dispatch --local
217	}
218

221	  line=$(_entire_session_fzf 'down:70%:wrap') || return
222	  [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
223	  session_id=$(cut -f1 <<< "$line")
224	  agent=$(cut -f3 <<< "$line")

file.content:
local line session_id agent checkpoint_id action

  line=$(_entire_session_fzf) || return
  [[ -n "$line" ]] || return

  session_id=$(cut -f1 <<< "$line")
  agent=$(cut -f3 <<< "$line" | xargs)
  action=$(_entire_action_pick | awk -F ':' '{gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", $1); print $1}') || return

  case "$action" in
    resume)
      _entire_open_agent "$session_id" "$agent"
      ;;
    explain)
      checkpoint_id=$(_entire_latest_checkpoint_id_by_session "$session_id") || return
      command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
      ;;
    checkpoints)
      checkpoint_id=$(_entire_checkpoint_pick_by_session "$session_id") || return
      command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
